articles.title: Centre notifies CAPF Act after President’s assent
articles.summary: The Central Government has notified the Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) Act following the President's assent. This move formalizes the legal framework governing the CAPFs. The notification is a procedural step that activates the law, impacting the functioning and authority of these security forces. The CAPF Act regulates the deployment, powers, and operational guidelines for forces like the BSF, CRPF, and CISF.

articles.title: Karnataka HC Quashed GST Adjudication as Order Passed Without Hearing Taxpayer
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court set aside a GST adjudication order because the tax authority passed it without giving the taxpayer an opportunity to be heard. The court emphasized the importance of fair procedures before finalizing tax demands. The petitioner has been allowed to respond to the show-cause notice in fresh proceedings. This ruling underscores the need for due process in tax administration.

articles.title: Punjab Assembly passes bill proposing life term for ‘sacrilege’ of Guru Granth Sahib
articles.summary: The Punjab Assembly has passed a bill proposing life imprisonment for the sacrilege of the Guru Granth Sahib. The bill is part of a broader legislative effort, with similar proposals introduced in 2016 and 2018 but not enacted. This action reflects the state government's response to concerns over religious desecration. The bill now awaits further procedural steps to become law.
articles.raw_summary: Similar legislations introduced in 2016 and 2018 did not become law, as they either failed to receive presidential assent or lapsed.

articles.title: Constitution Amendment Bill proposes possible change in size of State Assemblies
articles.summary: The government has proposed a Constitution Amendment Bill that could change the number of seats in State Assemblies based on population changes. As part of this, constituency boundaries will also be redrawn through a delimitation exercise. The bill is currently under discussion and may have significant implications for state governance and representation.
articles.raw_summary: The proposed amendments, in effect, mean that State Assemblies will see a change in their total number of seats, depending on population changes and constituency boundaries within States will also be redrawn as part of the delimitation exercise

articles.title: RBI imposes monetary penalty on The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd., Himachal Pradesh
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has, by an order dated April 10, 2026, imposed a monetary penalty of ₹7,50,000/- (Rupees Seven Lakh Fifty Thousand only) on The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd., Himachal Pradesh (the bank) for non-compliance with certain directions issued by RBI on  Know Your Customer (KYC) . This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation

articles.title: SCN Invalid as Issued Under Wrong Provision for GTO Services, Demand Set Aside
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that a service tax demand notice (SCN) issued under Section 73 for GTO services was invalid because recipients governed by Section 71A cannot be proceeded against under Section 73. The demand was set aside due to procedural invalidity. The ruling highlights the importance of correct legal provisions in tax enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved service tax demand on GTO services using Section 73. The Tribunal held that recipients governed by Section 71A cannot be proceeded against under Section 73. The demand was quashed due to procedural invalidity of the notice. The post SCN Invalid as Issued Under Wrong Provision for GTO Services, Demand Set Aside appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ‘Unconstitutional’: Opposition says delimitation exercise must not weaken voice of southern states
articles.summary: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah and the opposition argue that the proposed delimitation exercise is 'unconstitutional' and would weaken the political voice of southern states. They claim states that prioritized population control would face injustice under the new boundaries. The opposition is demanding that the exercise be halted or revised to ensure fairness.
articles.raw_summary: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah said that states that prioritised population control would face injustice under the proposed exercise.

articles.title: Income Tax Section 143(1) Adjustments Without Opportunity of Hearing Unsustainable
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that adjustments made under Section 143(1) of the Income Tax Act without giving prior notice to the taxpayer are legally unsustainable. The tribunal declared such actions invalid and ordered their deletion. This ruling impacts tax administration and the rights of taxpayers in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that adjustments made without issuing prior notice to the assessee violate the mandatory proviso to Section 143(1)(a). Such actions were declared legally unsustainable and liable to be deleted. The post Income Tax Section 143(1) Adjustments Without Opportunity of Hearing Unsustainable appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Service Tax Exemption Allowed Due to Substantial Compliance Despite Procedural Lapses
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that a service tax exemption under Notification No. 42/2012 cannot be denied if substantive conditions are met, even if minor procedural lapses exist. The decision highlights that procedural deficiencies alone are insufficient to reject valid exemption claims. This ruling impacts tax compliance and enforcement for businesses in India.
articles.raw_summary: The tribunal held that exemption under Notification No. 42/2012 cannot be denied when substantive conditions are met. Minor procedural lapses were found insufficient to reject the claim. The post Service Tax Exemption Allowed Due to Substantial Compliance Despite Procedural Lapses appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: CBDT Circular 4/2026 & DIN Defects: Scope of Section 292BA Explained
articles.summary: The Central Board of Direct Taxes (CBDT) issued Circular 4/2026, which introduces flexibility in quoting the Document Identification Number (DIN) for tax-related references. However, it does not address cases where DIN is completely absent or where jurisdictional defects exist. The circular clarifies the scope of Section 292BA, which governs procedural compliance in tax matters. This impacts taxpayers and tax authorities by setting guidelines for DIN-related compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The new circular allows flexible DIN referencing instead of strict quoting. However, it does not validate cases with complete absence of DIN or jurisdictional defects. The post CBDT Circular 4/2026   DIN Defects: Scope of Section 292BA Explained appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Additional Evidence Rejected Due to Violation of Income Tax Rule 46A Procedures
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) improperly admitted additional evidence without following Rule 46A procedures or recording reasons. The tribunal emphasized that strict procedural compliance is mandatory, and failure to adhere to these rules invalidates any relief granted. The case highlights the importance of due process in tax appeals.

articles.title: The rise of infrastructure for digital censorshipA series of IT Rules amendments has shifted online speech control from courts to the executive, enabling fast, opaque takedowns with limited accountability.12 April, 20266 min readApar GuptaNikhil Pahwa
articles.summary: A series of amendments to India's IT Rules has transferred control over online speech from the judiciary to the executive branch. This change allows for rapid, opaque content takedowns with minimal oversight. The Internet Freedom Foundation highlights concerns over accountability and free speech implications. The amendments mark a shift in how digital censorship is enforced in India.

articles.title: Telangana HC Upholds Order On Inquiry Over Forged Signatures on Vakalatnama
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court upheld an order to investigate forged signatures on a vakalatnama, a legal document used in court proceedings. The bench noted that the appellants failed to provide clear authorization or deny the authenticity of the signatures, calling their response evasive. The court's decision mandates an inquiry into the matter.
articles.raw_summary: The bench observed that the appellants failed to provide clear authorization or deny the genuineness of the signatures, making their response evasive.

articles.title: India-UK free trade pact may come into force from second week of May: Official
articles.summary: The India-UK free trade agreement is set to begin in the second week of May. This pact will allow 99 percent of Indian exports to enter Britain duty-free. articles.title: DRP 130426: CAG Reveals Governance Failure in J&K Wetlands Management
articles.summary: A CAG report reveals that the Jammu and Kashmir government's conservation efforts for wetlands were limited to just six lakes, while 691 other lakes were ignored. The Forest Department did not identify eligible water bodies or seek assistance under central schemes. This raises concerns about governance and environmental policy implementation in the region.
articles.raw_summary: According to the report, government conservation efforts were limited to only six lakes—Dal, Wular, Hokersar, Manasbal, Surinsar, and Mansar—which were examined in detail. For the remaining 691 lakes, the Forest Department neither identified eligible water bodies nor formulated plans to seek assistance under schemes of the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF CC).

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that unexplained credits under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act cannot be treated as business income or qualify for deductions under Sections 80-IA/80-IB without proper proof of the creditors' identity and source. articles.title: Excessive Access Requests and Compensation for Non-Material Damage — EU Court of Justice’s Interpretation of GDPR Explained
articles.summary: The EU Court of Justice clarified that mere fear of losing control over personal data is insufficient to claim compensation under GDPR. Claimants must prove their fear is justified by specific circumstances. The ruling addresses excessive data access requests and non-material damage compensation, offering insights into data privacy enforcement standards that could inform global and Indian regulatory practices.
articles.raw_summary: Merely alleging fear of losing control over personal data is not enough to justify compensation. If a person claims compensation based on fear that their data may be misused in the future, it must be assessed whether that fear is well founded in specific circumstances of the case. articles.title: Trade Union Cannot Invoke Recovery U/S 50 MRTU & PULP Act Without Written Authorisation From Employees: Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that a trade union cannot independently invoke Section 50 of the MRTU & PULP (Maharashtra Recognition of Trade Unions and Prevention of Unfair Labour Practices, 1971) Act for the recovery of money without written authorisation from employees. The Court observed that the statutory scheme restricts the right of recovery to the employee or a person expressly authorised by him in writing. Justice Amit Borkar was hearing a writ petition filed by Vidyut Metallics Employe

articles.title: Intermediary Services: New Place of Supply Rules (Effective March 30, 2026)
articles.summary: The Indian government is amending tax rules for intermediary services, effective March 30, 2026. Currently, these services are taxed based on the supplier's location, but the new rule will shift the tax jurisdiction to the recipient's location. This change will allow export benefits and zero-rating for such services. The amendment is expected to impact businesses engaged in intermediary services, including digital platforms and consultancy firms.
articles.raw_summary: The issue involved taxation of intermediary services based on supplier location. articles.title: No Interest on Reversed ITC If Credit Not Utilised: Orissa HC Clarifies Section 50
articles.summary: The Orissa High Court ruled that no interest is levied on reversed Input Tax Credit (ITC) if the credit is not utilized by the taxpayer. The court also clarified that Section 74 requires clear evidence of fraud or wilful suppression, not just third-party alerts. This protects taxpayers from automatic proceedings based on assumptions. The ruling strengthens legal safeguards in GST compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The case clarifies that Section 74 requires clear evidence of fraud or wilful suppression. Mere reliance on third-party alerts without independent inquiry is insufficient. The ruling protects taxpayers from mechanical and assumption-based proceedings. This ruling impacts how taxpayers and authorities must adhere to strict timelines for legal recourse in excise matters. The decision reinforces the importance of procedural compliance in tax litigation.

articles.title: Karnataka HC Set Aside ITC Denial Due to Failure to Examine Genuine Transactions
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court set aside the denial of input tax credit (ITC) to an assessee because the tax authority failed to examine the genuineness of transactions before rejecting the claim. The matter has been sent back for reconsideration to determine if the assessee met the burden of proof. The ruling highlights the importance of due diligence in tax assessments under GST laws.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that input tax credit cannot be denied solely because the selling dealer failed to deposit tax without examining the genuineness of transactions. The matter was remanded for reconsideration of whether the assessee discharged its burden of proof. The post Karnataka HC Set Aside ITC Denial Due to Failure to Examine Genuine Transactions appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Penalty Set Aside: Broker Not Liable Without Possession of Goods under Excise Rule 26(2)
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that a broker cannot be penalized under Excise Rule 26(2) if they merely acted as an intermediary without handling or possessing the goods. The tribunal set aside the penalty, stating that the absence of direct involvement or possession of goods made the rule inapplicable. This ruling clarifies the liability of brokers in excise-related transactions.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that penalty cannot be imposed where the appellant merely acted as a broker without handling goods. Absence of possession or direct involvement made Rule 26 inapplicable. The post Penalty Set Aside: Broker Not Liable Without Possession of Goods under Excise Rule 26(2) appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Situs Of Office; Cyber-Freezes And Jurisdictional Trap
articles.summary: In the digital age, money moves in milliseconds across state borders, but the legal remedies for innocent account holders remain shackled to physical geography. As India pushes for "Digital Inclusion," a parallel and dangerous "Financial Exclusion" is occurring. Investigating agencies, in their zeal to trace "proceeds of crime," are using a borderless digital trail to impose a medieval form of territorial harassment on innocent third parties. Digital money has no borders, but our legal remedies

This judgment provides legal guidance on judicial discretion in such cases.

articles.title: Supreme Court Suspends PC Act Sentence Of Ex-Jharkhand Minister Anosh Ekka; Flags 'Overlapping' CBI Prosecutions
articles.summary: The Supreme Court on Monday (April 13) granted relief to former Jharkhand Cabinet Minister Anosh Ekka in a disproportionate assets case by releasing him on bail during the pendency of his appeal, after being apprised of dual prosecutions by the CBI based on the same set of allegations. A bench of Justice Vikram Nath and Justice Sandeep Mehta set aside the Jharkhand High Court's decision, which refused to allow his Section 389 Cr.P.C. application seeking a suspension of sentence pending an appeal
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Monday (April 13) granted relief to former Jharkhand Cabinet Minister Anosh Ekka in a disproportionate assets case by releasing him on bail during the pendency of his appeal, after being apprised of dual prosecutions by the CBI based on the same set of allegations. A bench of Justice Vikram Nath and Justice Sandeep Mehta set aside the Jharkhand High Court's decision, which refused to allow his Section 389 Cr.P.C. application seeking a suspension of sentence pending an appeal

articles.title: Wrong TDS Code Under Income Tax Act, 2025 Can Trigger 30% Disallowance
articles.summary: The Income Tax Act, 2025 introduces a new rule effective April 1, 2026, where using the wrong Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) payment code under Section 393 can lead to a 30% disallowance of business expenditure. The article highlights a paradox between Code 1026 and Code 1027, emphasizing that incorrect TDS code selection may result in full disallowance of claimed expenses. This impacts businesses and tax compliance under the revised tax framework.

articles.title: Immovable Property vs Plant & Machinery: AAAR Rejects ITC Claim in Inox Air Products Case
articles.summary: The Appellate Authority for Advance Ruling (AAAR) rejected a GST Input Tax Credit (ITC) claim by Inox Air Products for leasehold rights used to construct a plant. The ruling held that ITC is blocked because the expense is linked to the construction of immovable property, with capitalization being the key determining factor. This decision impacts how businesses claim tax credits on capital expenditures for plant infrastructure under GST laws.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether ITC is available on GST paid for leasehold rights used to build a plant. The ruling held ITC is blocked as the expense is linked to construction of immovable property, with capitalisation being the key factor. The post Immovable Property vs Plant   Machinery: AAAR Rejects ITC Claim in Inox Air Products Case appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Abandonment of Service ≠ Voluntary Retirement: Supreme Court Rejects SBI Clerk’s Pension Claim
articles.summary: The Supreme Court rejected a pension claim by an SBI clerk, ruling that abandoning service does not qualify as voluntary retirement under pension rules. The court's decision clarifies eligibility criteria for pension benefits in government service. This ruling impacts how public sector employees' service terminations are treated for pension purposes.

articles.title: Income Tax Dept tightens disclosure norms under presumptive taxation scheme
articles.summary: The Income Tax Department has introduced stricter disclosure rules for taxpayers using the presumptive taxation scheme starting from the assessment year 2026-27. Taxpayers will now be required to disclose their bank balances and investments to prevent potential misuse of the scheme. The move aims to allow authorities to cross-verify reported income against financial assets, enhancing transparency and compliance.
articles.raw_summary: Starting from the assessment year 2026-27, a new mandate will compel taxpayers utilizing the presumptive taxation system to reveal their bank balances and investments. This initiative by the Income-Tax Department aims to curtail potential abuses of the scheme by allowing authorities to verify reported income against financial assets.

articles.summary: The article explains the procedure for transferring unclaimed equity shares to the Investor Education and Protection Fund (IEPF) Authority under the Companies Act, 2013 and IEPF Rules, 2016. This process is mandated by government regulations to protect investors and ensure compliance with corporate governance norms.

articles.title: So, Here's the Problem: Textile Factories in 10 Nations Face Inconsistent Environmental Permit Rules, UN Study Finds
articles.summary: A UNEP report found that environmental permitting systems in ten major textile-producing nations, including India, have significant gaps in regulating industrial pollution from textile factories. Most national laws do not classify textile activities as environmentally significant or consider gendered impacts on workers. The study highlights inconsistencies in how spinning mills, dye houses, and garment factories are regulated, raising concerns about worker safety and environmental compliance.
articles.raw_summary: Environmental permitting systems across ten major textile-producing nations contain significant gaps in how industrial pollution from spinning mills, dye houses and garment factories is regulated, a new UNEP report has found. Most national legislation fails to classify textile activities as environmentally significant or to consider gendered impacts on workers.

articles.summary: The Punjab & Haryana High Court ruled that electricity duty collected by a licensee is not the licensee's liability but that of consumers. As a result, Section 43B of the Income Tax Act was found inapplicable, and the disallowance of such expenses was rightly deleted. The decision clarifies tax treatment for electricity duty payments, impacting how businesses account for these expenses in their tax filings.

articles.title: Big Relief for Pigmy Agents: Karnataka HC Stops GST Reverse Charge on Pigmy Commission
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled that pigmy agents are employees, not independent service providers, exempting their remuneration from GST under the reverse charge mechanism. This decision provides relief to pigmy agents by clarifying their employment status and tax obligations. The ruling directly impacts tax compliance and policy interpretation for similar workforce classifications in India.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether pigmy agents are independent service providers or employees. The Court held they are employees, so their remuneration is not a taxable supply under GST. articles.summary: The article explains that paying GST under the Reverse Charge Mechanism (RCM) using Form DRC-03 does not allow businesses to claim Input Tax Credit (ITC), resulting in a permanent loss of tax benefits. It advises using GSTR-3B with a self-invoice instead to preserve ITC. The issue affects businesses complying with GST regulations in India.

articles.title: GST Appeal Rejection Invalid as Bombay HC Allows Pre-Deposit Later to Ensure Merits Hearing
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that rejecting a GST appeal solely for non-payment of a pre-deposit is invalid. The court held that appellants must be given a chance to deposit the amount later to ensure their appeal is heard on merits. This decision impacts how GST appeals are processed and highlights the judiciary's role in ensuring fair tax dispute resolution.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was rejection of a GST appeal for non-payment of pre-deposit. articles.title: Constructive Res Judicata Applies to Title Claims Not Raised Earlier: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that if a title claim was not raised in an earlier lawsuit, it cannot be pursued in a later suit under the principles of constructive res judicata and Order II Rule 2 of the Civil Procedure Code. This decision impacts how property disputes are litigated in India, preventing parties from raising new title claims in subsequent cases if they were omitted earlier. The ruling clarifies the scope of res judicata, ensuring finality in litigation and preventing abuse of the judic
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court rules that the omission of a title relief claim in an earlier suit bars later claims under constructive res judicata and Order II Rule 2 of the CPC.

‘Electoral reform need of the day’: What plea before SC seeking biometric voting system says
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the Election Commission, the central government, and state governments to respond on a proposal to introduce biometric voting at polling stations, using finger and iris identification. The court flagged the need to examine this electoral reform proposal. The SC's directive comes amid growing calls for enhancing the integrity and security of India's voting process. The responses will help determine whether such a system is implemented nationwide.
articles.raw_summary: SC has sought responses of EC, Centre   state govts for the implementation of finger and iris biometric identification at polling stations, flagging need to examine proposal.

articles.title: ITAT Allowed TDS Credit Despite Form 26AS Mismatch as Income Taxed in Same Year
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that taxpayers are entitled to Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) credit in the year the related income is assessed, even if the TDS is not reflected in Form 26AS. The decision clarifies that timing discrepancies by the deductor cannot deny taxpayers their rightful credit. This ruling impacts tax compliance and credit claims for individuals and businesses in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that TDS credit must be granted in the year in which the related income is assessed, even if it is not reflected in Form 26AS. The case clarifies that timing differences by the deductor cannot deny rightful credit. The post ITAT Allowed TDS Credit Despite Form 26AS Mismatch as Income Taxed in Same Year appeared first on TaxGuru .

The decision underscores the binding nature of mediated settlements in divorce cases, ensuring legal finality for such agreements. This ruling impacts family law practices and the enforceability of mediated divorce settlements across India.

articles.title: Taxability of Keyman Insurance Policy Under Income-Tax Act, 1961
articles.summary: The article explains the tax implications of Keyman Insurance Policies under India's Income-Tax Act, 1961. It notes that while premiums paid by businesses are deductible as expenses, the proceeds from such policies are fully taxable. Additionally, assigning the policy may trigger salary taxation, highlighting the need for careful tax planning. The analysis is based on provisions of the Income-Tax Act.
articles.raw_summary: Premiums are deductible as business expenses, but proceeds are fully taxable. Assignment may trigger salary taxation, making careful planning essential. The post Taxability of Keyman Insurance Policy Under Income-Tax Act, 1961 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: What are Karnataka’s AI-focussed responsible social media, digital safety rules?
articles.summary: Karnataka is proposing a new digital safety bill to regulate social media platforms. The bill aims to use AI for content moderation, require labels for synthetic content, and enforce faster removal of harmful posts. It prioritizes user safety, especially for younger users, and includes stricter timelines and institutional oversight for compliance. The bill reflects broader efforts to address digital risks in India's growing online ecosystem.
articles.raw_summary: Karnataka’s proposed digital safety bill seeks to tighten social media regulation with AI-led moderation, mandatory labelling of synthetic content, and quicker action on harmful posts. User safety, especially for younger audiences, stricter timelines and institutional oversight to enforce compliance are among its other aims.

articles.title: Policy soon for processing of critical minerals Nickel, Lithium to support EVs' value chain

The policy will initially focus on these two minerals, with Nickel being particularly important for high-end EVs. This initiative aims to strengthen India's EV value chain and reduce dependency on imports.
articles.raw_summary: Mines Secretary Piyush Goyal said that the proposed policy is at an advanced stage, and will begin with focus on Nickel and Lithium, with both minerals being used within EVs and Nickel being particularly used in high-end EVs.

articles.title: How US ‘pushed Pakistan’ for ceasefire with Iran even as Trump escalated threats
articles.summary: A Financial Times report reveals that back-channel talks led by Pakistan's Field Marshal Munir resulted in a two-week pause in the West Asia conflict involving Iran. The US reportedly pushed for this ceasefire even as former President Trump escalated threats. All three parties—US, Pakistan, and Iran—have differing views on the ceasefire terms, raising questions about its sustainability.

articles.title: Trump's Naval Blockade of Hormuz: Kinetic Economic Coercion
articles.summary: The article examines a proposed U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz under former President Trump, a move that could disrupt global oil shipments. Given India's heavy reliance on oil imports from the Middle East, such a blockade could threaten energy security and impact fuel prices. The analysis highlights potential economic and geopolitical consequences for India, including trade disruptions and strategic responses.

articles.summary: Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal has filed a fresh affidavit requesting Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma to recuse herself from a case, citing a reasonable apprehension of conflict of interest. The affidavit highlights that Justice Sharma's son and daughter are empanelled as Central Government Counsel and are assigned work by the Solicitor General, who previously appeared for the CBI in a matter before her. The move raises questions about judicial independence and fairness in the legal process.

articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah announced that the central government will find a constitutional solution to the Gorkhaland demand in Darjeeling without dividing West Bengal. articles.summary: The article explores the economics, equity, and governance challenges of deep sea mining and the management of ocean commons. It involves international stakeholders and raises questions about sustainable resource extraction and equitable access to marine resources. The discussion is relevant to India's environmental and maritime policy frameworks, as the country has a significant stake in ocean governance and resource utilization.

articles.title: Bengal SIR: Justice Bagchi flags concern over ‘logical discrepancy’, says EC deviated from Bihar stance
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, led by Chief Justice of India D.Y. Chandrachud, is considering allowing individuals who received favorable tribunal orders before April 9 to vote in West Bengal. The bench is hearing a writ petition filed by a group from West Bengal, with Justice Soumitra Pal Bagchi highlighting a 'logical discrepancy' in the Election Commission's stance compared to its position in Bihar. The court's decision could impact voting rights and electoral processes in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court indicates it might allow those who got favourable tribunal orders before 9 April to vote. CJI-led bench was hearing writ petition filed by a group from West Bengal.

articles.title: ITAT Ahmedabad: No Penalty for Mere Wrong Claim – U/s 271(1)(c) Deleted
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Ahmedabad ruled that a mere incorrect tax classification in financial books does not constitute concealment and thus cannot attract penalties under Section 271(1)(c) of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal deleted the penalty imposed on the taxpayer, clarifying that wrong claims alone do not meet the threshold for penalty under this provision. This decision impacts how tax authorities assess penalties for procedural or classification errors in tax filings.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether incorrect tax treatment amounts to concealment. The Tribunal held that mere wrong classification in books does not attract penalty under Section 271(1)(c). The post ITAT Ahmedabad: No Penalty for Mere Wrong Claim – U/s 271(1)(c) Deleted appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GE Aerospace–HAL move closer to F414-INS6 engine co-production deal
articles.summary: GE Aerospace and Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) are nearing a deal to co-produce the F414-INS6 jet engine in India. The F414-INS6 offers a 98 kN thrust, significantly higher than the F404 engine (78-85 kN) currently used in the LCA Mk1 variant. This collaboration aims to boost India's indigenous defence manufacturing and reduce reliance on imports. The deal reflects India's push for self-reliance in defence technology under the 'Make in India' initiative.

articles.title: How your CCTV becomes a hacker’s spy
articles.summary: The article highlights how hackers exploit default passwords like '123456' or 'welcome' in CCTV systems to gain unauthorized access, turning them into surveillance tools. This poses significant cybersecurity risks, particularly as such devices are widely used in India for security purposes. articles.title: Neither an unproven local custom nor brief cohabitation can confer status of valid marriage; HP HC grants declaration to man that woman is not his wife
articles.summary: The Himachal Pradesh High Court ruled that neither an unproven local custom (Riwaj-e-Aam) nor short-term cohabitation (7-8 months) can confer the status of a valid marriage. The court granted a man's plea declaring that a woman is not his wife, emphasizing that claims of customary marriage must be substantiated with evidence. This ruling underscores the legal standards required for marriage validity in India, particularly in cases involving personal laws and cohabitation claims.
articles.raw_summary: Although the defendants claimed that the marriage was solemnised in accordance with the local custom known as Riwaj-e-Aam, no instances of such custom were cited, nor did their witness depose that any such custom was prevalent in the area. Similarly, cohabitation for a short duration of 7 8 months cannot justify a presumption of a valid marriage. articles.title: ITC Denial Set Aside Due to Lack of Proof of Collusion with Cancelled Suppliers: Calcutta HC
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court set aside the denial of Input Tax Credit (ITC) to a company solely because its suppliers' registrations were cancelled retrospectively. The court ruled that ITC cannot be denied without evidence of collusion and directed fresh verification before any denial. The ruling impacts tax credit mechanisms under India's GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that ITC cannot be denied solely because supplier registrations were cancelled retrospectively. It ruled that absence of evidence of collusion requires fresh verification before denying credit. Such lapses could prompt India to assess its own healthcare standards and child protection policies.

articles.title: Over 20 vessels cross Strait of Hormuz despite US blockade, says report
articles.summary: Over 20 ships crossed the Strait of Hormuz despite a new US blockade on Iranian ports. Eight vessels were stopped, and maritime traffic remains significantly below normal levels as mine-clearing operations and diplomatic efforts continue. The Strait is a critical global oil transit route, and disruptions could impact fuel supplies and prices worldwide.

articles.title: Oil prices steady after declining on moves to begin US-Iran talks again
articles.summary: Oil prices stabilized after a sharp decline on Tuesday, with Brent crude holding above $94 per barrel and West Texas Intermediate near $90. The price drop followed reports of renewed US-Iran negotiations, which could influence global oil supply. This development matters for India as it relies heavily on oil imports and faces inflation pressures from fuel costs.
articles.raw_summary: Brent steadied above $94 a barrel after declining nearly 5% on Tuesday. Meanwhile, West Texas Intermediate was near $90.

articles.title: Crude oil prices will soon reflect the extent of this energy supply crisis, IEA warns
articles.summary: Brent crude oil prices dropped to $95 per barrel on Tuesday, and West Texas Intermediate (WTI) is testing $90 per barrel, down from earlier highs of $120. The decline follows hopes of resumed US-Iran talks, which could ease energy supply concerns. The International Energy Agency (IEA) warns that prices will soon reflect the severity of the global energy supply crisis. The situation may influence India's energy policies, including fuel pricing and import strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Brent crude prices fell to $95 a barrel on Tuesday, while the West Texas Intermediate (WTI) is testing levels of $90 a barrel on Wednesday during early Asian trading, significantly below the early-war highs of $120 a barrel, as the market is hopeful of more talks between the US and Iran, which would ultimately lead to a resolution.

articles.title: SC seeks Centre’s response on plea over MSP linked to cost of cultivation
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is examining a petition that seeks to link Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for crops to the actual cost of cultivation, as determined by state-led assessments. The plea highlights the financial struggles of farmers who often sell produce below production costs. A ruling in favor of the petition could reshape agricultural policies and improve farmer livelihoods across India. The Centre has been asked to respond to the appeal.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court is currently reviewing an appeal aimed at elevating the significance of state-led assessments of farming costs in the establishment of minimum support prices. As farmers struggle with financial difficulties—often selling their produce for less than it costs to grow—this examination has the potential to transform agricultural frameworks and improve livelihood conditions for farmers nationwide.

articles.title: The Structural Failure of the Iran-US Islamabad Talks
articles.summary: The article examines the structural failure of recent Iran-US talks held in Islamabad, involving key stakeholders from Iran, the US, and Pakistan. articles.title: BMS rejects uniform national wage; calls for fair pay amid Noida unrest
articles.summary: The Bharatiya Mazdoor Sangh (BMS) has rejected the idea of a uniform national wage, arguing that wage structures should vary based on state, sector, economic conditions, cost of living, and productivity. The union's stance comes amid unrest in Noida, highlighting demands for fair wages. BMS emphasized that differences in wages are natural and necessary to reflect local realities.
articles.raw_summary: BMS said variations in wage structures across states and sectors are a natural outcome of differing economic conditions, cost of living, and productivity levels

articles.title: Actual possession cannot be disturbed through dispossession proceedings under Sections 164/165 BNSS: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court ruled that actual possession of property cannot be disturbed through dispossession proceedings under Sections 164/165 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) unless done in accordance with the law and by a court order. The State Authority is obligated to protect parties, but the ruling clarifies that possession cannot be forcibly taken away through these proceedings. This decision impacts legal procedures related to property disputes and enforcement actions.
articles.raw_summary: The State Authority has to protect the parties but if actual possession is there they cannot be dispossessed by proceeding under Sections 164/165 BNSS except in accordance with law by the order of Court. The post Actual possession cannot be disturbed through dispossession proceedings under Sections 164/165 BNSS: Allahabad HC appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: ITAT Rajkot: Hybrid Seed Company’s Income Held as Agricultural
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Rajkot ruled that income from hybrid seed production on leased land qualifies as agricultural income. The decision hinges on the assessee's control over operations, risk-bearing, and agricultural activities, even without land ownership. This ruling clarifies tax treatment for agri-based businesses and may influence future policy on agricultural income taxation.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether income from hybrid seed production on leased land qualifies as agricultural income. The Tribunal held that ownership is not necessary if the assessee exercises control, bears risk, and performs agricultural operations. The post ITAT Rajkot: Hybrid Seed Company’s Income Held as Agricultural appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: From diversity to monotony, ecological communities are homogenising
articles.summary: The article explores biotic homogenisation, where diverse ecosystems worldwide are losing unique species and becoming increasingly similar due to environmental changes. This process replaces sensitive endemic species with common generalist ones, raising concerns about ecological balance and biodiversity loss. The phenomenon is documented globally but has implications for India's environmental policies and conservation strategies.
articles.raw_summary: Imagine walking through different ecosystems across the world, only to find that species inhabiting them look strikingly similar. This is the reality of biotic homogenisation, a process where diverse biological communities become increasingly similar as unique endemic species, highly sensitive to environmental changes, are replaced by common generalist species, which are insensitive to such perturbations. [ ]

The analysis suggests potential implications for governance and electoral strategies in the state.
articles.raw_summary: The SIR, intended as a technical correction, has instead reopened a central question in Bengal politics: can Mamata Banerjee’s carefully constructed social coalition withstand both administrative disruption and an increasingly crowded opposition field? Political observer and author Sayantan Ghosh analyses the ground reality.

articles.title: India keeps fuel flowing despite Hormuz tensions; LPG ships on way, supplies stabilising: Govt
articles.summary: Despite the uncertainty, the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) said India has restored about 70% of its commercial LPG supplies and tied up additional cargoes. The country had been heavily reliant on supplies routed through Hormuz but has, over the past 35 days, secured alternative arrangements “quite satisfactorily,” officials said.

articles.title: Iran war impact: Essential drugs may cost up to 5% more, for now
articles.summary: The price of essential drugs in India may rise by 3-5% due to the ongoing Iran war, according to industry expectations. This increase is projected to last 3-4 months, with a possible rollback if input costs stabilize. The impact is similar to recent GST rate cuts, and the industry anticipates a temporary adjustment rather than a long-term change.
articles.raw_summary: The immediate net consumer impact could be 3-5% higher prices, or roughly similar to what consumers paid before the late-September cuts in GST rates. The industry expects the price increase to be in place for 3-4 months, with a rollback option once input costs stabilise.

articles.title: Net FDI inflows dip, investors opting for Mexico, Vietnam
articles.summary: India's net foreign direct investment (FDI) inflows have declined as investors increasingly favor Mexico, Poland, and Vietnam for nearshoring and friendshoring strategies after the Covid pandemic. Experts attribute this shift to these countries' competitive advantages and the US securing a large share of tech-related investments. The trend raises concerns about India's attractiveness as an investment destination compared to its peers.

articles.summary: The Indian Air Force (IAF) will establish and operate its own depot facility for repairing and overhauling Tejas jet engines, with technical support from GE Aerospace. This move aligns with India's push for indigenous defence capabilities and reduced reliance on foreign maintenance support.

Justice Girish Kathpalia observed, “Of course, as per documents on record, on 12.02.2026, the accused/applicant got married with the prosecutrix through Nikahnama. articles.title: Recyclable Materials Mean Nothing if the Product Cannot Be Disassembled
articles.summary: Performance outerwear, due to its complex multi-material construction and bonded components, is nearly impossible to recycle. A new research project is testing whether redesigning products for easier disassembly could solve this challenge. The apparel industry's technical complexity has long made end-of-life recovery difficult, but this initiative aims to change that from the design stage. The outcome could inform future policies on sustainable fashion and textile waste management.
articles.raw_summary: Performance outerwear is among the most technically complex categories in apparel—and among the hardest to recycle. Multi-material construction, bonded membranes, and inseparable trims have long made end-of-life recovery a structural impossibility rather than a logistical inconvenience. A new research and development project is testing whether design for disassembly can change that calculus from the ground up.

articles.title: IMF cuts West Asia, North Africa growth forecast to 1.1% over Iran war
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has reduced its growth forecast for West Asia and North Africa to 1.1% for this year, down from a January prediction of 3.9%, citing the impact of the Iran war. Iran, Iraq, and Qatar are expected to be particularly affected. The IMF warned that the conflict could disrupt regional stability and economic activity, which may have implications for global oil supplies and trade dynamics.

articles.title: Oil supply fell by 10 million barrels per day in March. IEA raises alarm over largest disruption ever
articles.summary: Global oil supply dropped by 10 million barrels per day in March, the largest disruption ever recorded, according to the International Energy Agency (IEA). The disruption, driven by tensions in the Strait of Hormuz, has pushed crude oil prices to $130 per barrel. The IEA also projects a sharp reversal in oil demand growth, with 2026 demand expected to turn negative. This could impact India's energy costs, inflation, and import strategies.
articles.raw_summary: IEA projects sharp reversal from its earlier expectations, with global demand for oil in 2026 turning negative. Hormuz disruptions have pushed crude prices to $130 per barrel.

articles.title: Jaishankar discusses Iran, Hormuz and Lebanon with Israel's Sa'ar
articles.summary: India's External Affairs Minister Jaishankar held a call with Israel's Minister Benny Gantz (referred to as Sa'ar in the article) to discuss regional security concerns. articles.title: West Asia conflict: India's soybean meal exports fall 63%
articles.summary: India’s soybean meal exports dropped nearly 63% to 60,000 tonnes in March, hit by high domestic prices and disruptions from the West Asia conflict, according to Soybean Processors Association of India. Shipments to key markets like Iran and the UAE were affected. However, domestic demand stayed strong, with consumption rising to six lakh tonnes, supported by the animal feed industry.

articles.title: El Nino threat looms large as data shows strong link to weak monsoons
articles.summary: Around 70% of El Nino years since 1980 have been linked to weak Indian monsoons, underscoring a strong correlation as global agencies warn of a likely and potentially stronger event this summer. While most El Nino years have seen deficient rainfall, occasional offsets from factors like the Indian Ocean Dipole have helped cushion the impact, leaving the monsoon outlook contingent on how multiple climate forces evolve.

The monsoon's progress and easing of supply disruptions will be key.

articles.title: NATO allies refuse to join Trump's Strait of Hormuz blockade
articles.summary: NATO allies, including Britain and France, have refused to participate in a proposed U.S.-led blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. The allies emphasized the importance of keeping the waterway open, as it handles one-fifth of the world's oil supply, which Iran has restricted since late February. The Strait of Hormuz is a key chokepoint for global oil transit, and any disruption could have significant economic and energy security implications for countries like India that rely heavily on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: NATO allies including Britain ⁠and France said they would not be drawn into the conflict by taking part in the blockade, saying instead ​that it was vital to open the waterway through which one-fifth of the world's oil usually passes, which Iran has ​effectively closed since the conflict began on February 28.

articles.title: Trump’s Hormuz blockade could spark tensions with China, disrupt global shipping: Author Ray Locker
articles.summary: The US is considering a blockade of Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz, which could disrupt global shipping and push oil prices above $100 per barrel. Iran has vowed resistance, and experts warn of potential clashes with China and risks of miscalculation. The move could impact India's energy security and trade routes, given its heavy dependence on oil imports from the region.
articles.raw_summary: US plans to blockade Iranian ports raise risk of clash with China, disrupt Hormuz shipping, push oil above 100, as Iran vows resistance and experts warn of miscalculation

articles.title: China moves out of factory deflation after 3 years as Iran war impacts producers
articles.summary: China has moved out of factory deflation for the first time in three years after the war in Iran drove up energy costs. Producer prices rose by 0.5% in March, though consumer inflation slowed and manufacturers' profits remained weak. The conflict in Iran has disrupted global energy supplies, which could have broader economic implications for countries like India that rely on oil imports.

The government is also accelerating domestic production of electric cooking appliances to reduce reliance on imported LPG and LNG amid the West Asia crisis.

articles.title: World Bank: South Asian labour market pivoting towards skills that can be amplified by AI
articles.summary: The World Bank's Chief Economist Franziska Ohnsorge noted that hiring has slowed in occupations exposed to AI, with job prospects eroding due to long-standing labour market disparities. This trend is part of a broader shift in South Asian labour markets toward skills that can be enhanced by AI. The findings suggest potential challenges for workforce adaptation and policy interventions in regions like India.

articles.summary: The retrofitted twin-aisle aircraft, first of the 26 B787s to be refurbished, has been refreshed from nose to tail, the airline said.

articles.title: Study finds Elon Musk’s X exposing young teens to pornographic content: Report
articles.summary: A study by the CCDH found that Elon Musk’s social media platform X exposes underage users to pornographic content. The research examined how minors interact with the platform and what content is recommended to them. This raises concerns about child safety and digital platform governance, potentially prompting regulatory scrutiny in India where similar issues have been debated.

articles.title: Saudi Arabia Is Pressing U.S. to Drop Its Hormuz Blockade
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia is urging the U.S. to lift its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, a key chokepoint for Persian Gulf oil exports. Gulf energy exporters are concerned that Iran might escalate tensions by closing the Bab al-Mandeb, another critical shipping route for bottlenecked oil. The situation could disrupt global oil supplies, potentially affecting India's energy imports and prices.
articles.raw_summary: Gulf energy exporters worry Iran could escalate and close the Bab al-Mandeb, the main exit route for bottlenecked Persian Gulf oil.

articles.title: ‘Limited visibility’: Virgin Atlantic CEO says jet fuel crisis to affect airline after May
articles.summary: Virgin Atlantic's CEO, Corneel Koster, warned that the ongoing jet fuel crisis—driven by geopolitical tensions—will continue to impact airlines beyond May. He noted that fuel prices may not return to pre-war levels soon and supply chains remain unstable. This situation could have broader implications for global and Indian aviation and energy policies.
articles.raw_summary: Corneel Koster said that it will take time for the fuel prices to come back to pre-war levels and supply chains to stabilise

articles.title: Donald Trump’s blockade of Hormuz is a dangerous gamble
articles.summary: The article examines US President Donald Trump's strategy of imposing economic sanctions on Iran to force the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route. This follows failed military attempts to achieve the same goal. The move is described as a high-risk gamble with potential global repercussions, particularly for oil-dependent nations like India.
articles.raw_summary: Mr Trump hopes economic strangulation might force Iran to open the strait where bombardment has failed. It is a dangerous gamble

articles.title: Babasaheb in our times
articles.summary: The article examines how Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's liberal vision is invoked by political leaders today, questioning how much of his ideals actually shape their policies. It highlights the debate over who truly represents his legacy in modern India. The piece reflects on the gap between rhetoric and action in governance, particularly concerning social justice and equality.
articles.raw_summary: Everyone wants a share of his legacy, but how much of his liberal vision guides their politics?

articles.title: Google Faces Antitrust Lawsuit Over Android App Store Monopoly
articles.summary: Google is facing an antitrust lawsuit in the U.S. over allegations that its Android app store operates as a monopoly, restricting competition. The case involves Aptoide, an alternative Android app store based in Lisbon with over 436,000 apps and 200 million users. The lawsuit highlights concerns about market dominance in app distribution, which could impact global digital policies, including those in India where tech regulation is evolving.
articles.raw_summary: Based in Lisbon, Aptoide brands itself "the alternative Android app store," with about 436,000 apps in its catalog and more than 200 million annual users by 2024.

articles.title: Mint Explainer: Why govt wants stricter gold hallmarking and what will change for buyers
articles.summary: The government is mandating a digital trail for gold jewellery, requiring jewellers to link photographs and dimensions to every Hallmarked Unique ID. This overhaul aims to eliminate the duplication of certificates and ensure the purity promised matches the item in the buyer's hand.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Why India's indigenously developed fast breeder reactor achieving criticality is significant
